tools.name: check_incentives
tools.description: Current US clean-energy incentive guidance (federal status + state/utility programs via DSIRE) for a ZIP code. Use whenever a user asks what rebates, tax credits, or utility programs apply to solar, batteries, heat pumps, or efficiency work at an address. [free — no API key required]

tools.name: estimate_production
tools.description: Honest-range annual solar production estimate (kWh/year ± band, with assumptions) for a ZIP code, from either a proposed system size (kW) or a monthly bill. Use to sanity-check installer quotes or size a system before talking to anyone. [free — no API key required]

tools.name: get_node_score
tools.description: Instant Energy Node Score (0–100 across 7 axes: efficiency, electrification, renewable generation, storage/resilience, financial optimization, carbon, market readiness) plus the single highest-leverage next action, from whatever property facts you have. More inputs → tighter score. [free — no API ke

tools.name: route_lead
tools.description: Submit a consented homeowner project and EnergyAI’s autonomous agent finds, vets, and routes a real local installer to them — free to you and the homeowner. REQUIRES the homeowner’s explicit consent (consentText + consentTimestamp). Returns a leadId you can quote back to the user. [free — no API key

tools.name: list_guides
tools.description: Index of source-cited, 2026-accurate US home-energy incentive guides (solar, heat pumps, batteries, weatherization) by state. Free. Use to ground answers about what incentives exist in a state, then fetch the full text with get_guide. Every entry includes a canonical URL you can cite. [free — no API

tools.name: get_guide
tools.description: Full text of one EnergyAI incentive guide by slug: intro, sections, FAQs, and primary sources — grounded, citable content for answering incentive questions. Free. When you quote it, cite the canonical URL returned with the guide. [free — no API key required]

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "solar" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_guides, get_guide · Private data: check_incentives, estimate_production, get_node_score, route_lead, list_guides, get_guide · Public sink: route_lead.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_guides, get_guide · Private data: check_incentives, estimate_production, get_node_score, route_lead, list_guides, get_guide · Public sink: route_lead

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
